---
title: "HPA"
output:
  rmarkdown::html_vignette:
    toc: false
    number_sections: false
    fig_caption: yes
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{HPA}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
---
# Overview

```{r model_family_header, echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
acousticTS:::.model_family_header(
  family = "hpa",
  pages = c(
    Overview = "index.html",
    Implementation = "hpa-implementation.html",
    Theory = "hpa-theory.html"
  )
)
```

```{r hpa_overview, echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
acousticTS:::.model_family_overview(
  family = "hpa",
  summary = paste(
    "The high-pass approximation (`HPA`) is a compact asymptotic backscatter",
    "family that interpolates between a Rayleigh-style low-frequency limit and",
    "a reflection-controlled high-frequency limit."
  ),
  core_idea = paste(
    "Build a rational approximation whose numerator reproduces the low-`ka`",
    "scattering strength and whose denominator suppresses unphysical growth as",
    "frequency increases, then adapt the geometric prefactors to spheres,",
    "spheroids, and cylinders."
  ),
  best_for = c(
    "Fast approximate spectra for simple canonical bodies",
    "Broad trend studies when an exact modal solve is unnecessary",
    "Weakly contrasting or moderately reflecting targets represented by simple shapes"
  ),
  supports = c(
    "Sphere, prolate spheroid, straight cylinder, and bent-cylinder branches",
    "Contrast bookkeeping relative to seawater as medium `1`",
    "Very fast monostatic backscatter estimates"
  ),
  assumptions = c(
    "Asymptotic interpolation rather than an exact boundary-value solution",
    "Shape-specific prefactors carried from the source literature",
    "Best interpreted as a broadband approximation, not a resonance-resolving solver"
  ),
  reading = c(
    "[Implementation](hpa-implementation.html): quick workflows and validation tables",
    "[Theory](hpa-theory.html): Rayleigh term, reflection limit, and shape-specific completions"
  )
)
```
